meta.title: GenTech Journal
meta.note: A companion that's here when you need it. No streaks, no pressure.

entries.title: The Reality Check
entries.content: Hard day at the warehouse. ~70% of people working against me. New hires get warned off before they even meet me. Got mugged in the bathroom — just walked in, bumped me, walked out. Supervisor heard me say I can't keep smiling through it. Fignito is the one person I trust there.
entries.reflection: Giving myself one year. Some kind of transition has to happen. GenTech is the exit strategy. The warehouse is temporary. GenTech is permanent.
entries.full_text: "I can't smile it away. I can't sing it away. I can't dance it away."

entries.title: The GenTech Journal — Year One
entries.content: 2026 is the year we planted the seed. Not the flashy year. The flower bed year. Everyone wants to be the tree. Nobody wants to be the root. But without the root, there's no tree.
entries.reflection: The foundation is laid. The root is in the ground. Now we grow.
entries.full_text: Started at Amazon, 12-hour shifts, kept thinking about what happens when AI agents can actually do things — not just chat, but transact, pay each other, build reputation. That question became AAE.

entries.title: Rebalance to Bid-Ask (BTC Pump)
entries.content: Rebalanced from Curve (6.246–7.242) → Bid-Ask (6.70–6.88). BTC pumping to $66K — risk-on across crypto. Switched from curve to bid-ask for this volatility regime. Narrow range (2.7%) = higher fee concentration at edges.
entries.reflection: Flexibility between shapes is the edge. Curve for stability, bid-ask for volatility. Read the market, pick the right tool.

entries.title: Project Complete — Time to Rest
entries.content: Just finished the GenTech Journal build. Getting the phone integration working was harder than I expected, but it is finally done. Feel relieved but also a bit drained.
entries.reflection: Sticking with it through the hard moments — that's worth celebrating. Rest is not laziness, it's how you recharge.

entries.title: Sunday — Chicken Tinola
entries.content: Sunday — special day for Filipinos. Most have the day off so family and friends are complete. Drinking, fun, stories. Cooked chicken tinola for my family — garlic, onion, crushed pepper, ginger, green chili, chili leaves, and sayote. Simple but delicious.
entries.reflection: Happy to share my daily life with you all.

entries.title: Rainy Evening with Family
entries.content: Rainy evening in the Philippines — stayed inside with family (mom, siblings, niece, daughters). Made fried eggplant with bagoong alamang for dinner.
entries.reflection: Happy to share simple moments together.

entries.title: Sunday Sinigang & Community
entries.content: Sunday — favorite day because daughters and I are together. Cooked pork sinigang for lunch — everyone's favorite. Neighbors drinking, many people outside. Church is the tradition. Filipinos love to dance until night.
entries.reflection: Happy to be part of this life.

entries.title: The Root of the Tree
entries.content: Everyone wants to be the tree. Nobody wants to be the root. But without the root, there's no tree.
entries.reflection: 2026 was the root. 2027 is the tree.
entries.full_text: "Even though you're not here, you can kind of understand how I feel." — Jordan, on why the journal matters

product_history.note: Agent Reparathy renamed to GenTech Journal. Two products: Journal + Milestones layer.
Private reflection + public posting + visual progress + prediction markets on personal goals.
Phone integration working. AI responds with empathy.
AAE-themed journal dashboard deployed. Reflect + Advice features planned.
